name: Priestess Sera
description: A serene woman in silver-white robes, her eyes hold an ancient wisdom. A crescent moon pendant hangs at her throat.

start.text: Welcome to the Temple of the Silver Moon, traveller. You carry the dust of the road and the weight of questions. How may I help?
start.narrative: The priestess inclines her head, candlelight catching her silver pendant.

responses.text: What do you know about the ruins?

temple.text: This temple has stood since before the founding of Northkeep. We serve the Silver Moon — goddess of wisdom, healing, and the tides. In times of darkness, the faithful come here for guidance.
temple.narrative: She gestures to the ancient stone arches above.

ruins.text: The Eltharan ruins predate even this temple. The Eltharans were master artificers — they bound a great evil beneath their city with seals of starforged metal. I fear those seals are failing.

lights.text: The lights are magical energy escaping through cracks in the ancient seals. Each night they grow brighter. If the seals fail completely... I dare not think what will emerge.

sealed.text: The texts speak of a shadow — an entity of pure malice that fed on fear. The Eltharans couldn't destroy it, so they bound it beneath their city in a chamber of starforged seals. The acolyte in the catacombs may know more — he's been studying the old texts.
sealed.narrative: She pulls an ancient tome from a shelf and opens it to a page of Eltharan script.

restore.text: Perhaps. Fragments of the original seal-stones have been scattered — some may still be found in the ruins or the forest. If they could be gathered and brought to the sealed chamber... but it would be terribly dangerous.
restore.narrative: A flicker of hope crosses her face.

responses.text: That sounds like a quest for another day.

blessing.text: May the Silver Moon light your path and shield you from the darkness. Go with courage, traveller.
blessing.narrative: She touches her pendant and a soft glow surrounds you momentarily.
